source.journal_name: Romanian Journal of Information Science and Technology

paper.title: A Frequent Construction Mining Scheme Based on Syntax Tree
paper.abstract: NLP construction mining from syntax trees. Proposes algorithm to extract frequent semantic constructions from phrase structure trees. Extends PrefixSpan algorithm for construction mining with gap handling. Corpus-based extraction of linguistic constructions.

verdict.summary: Pattern mining algorithm for NLP constructions. Definitions 1-7 (construction, constant, empty slot, phrase tree, tree sequence, support, frequent construction) are necessary for algorithm specification. Algorithm pseudocode and complexity analysis appropriate for data mining paper.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Sequential pattern mining for NLP constructions
**Level:** 3 (Algorithm design with theoretical foundations)
**Rationale:** Novel algorithm extending PrefixSpan for construction mining with gap handling.

### Necessity Test
- Definition 1 (Construction): Necessary - defines core mining target
- Definition 2-3 (Constant/Empty slot): Necessary - specifies pattern structure
- Definition 4 (Phrase structure tree): Necessary - defines input data structure
- Definition 5-7 (Tree sequence, Support, Frequent construction): Necessary - standard data mining formalisms
- Algorithm pseudocode: Necessary for reproducibility

**Verdict:** Formalism appropriate for algorithm design paper. Definitions are functional, not decorative.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Design and Assessment of an Anti-lock Braking System Controller
paper.abstract: ABS controller design using Simulink. Mathematical modeling of quarter-car ABS system. Proposes three-position regulator vs standard bang-bang controller. Simulation shows improved braking time and distance.

verdict.summary: Control systems engineering paper with appropriate formalism. Equations (1)-(7) for vehicle/wheel dynamics are standard control theory. Burckhardt tire model (Eq. 7) with empirical coefficients. Simulink model with experimental validation. Formalism is domain-standard and necessary.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** ABS controller design
**Level:** 3 (Control systems engineering)
**Rationale:** Novel three-position regulator for ABS with simulation validation.

### Necessity Test
- Eq. 1 (Force equilibrium): Necessary for vehicle dynamics
- Eq. 2 (Newton's second law): Fundamental physics
- Eq. 3-5 (Vehicle/wheel kinematics): Necessary for simulation model
- Eq. 6 (Wheel slip): Standard ABS definition
- Eq. 7 (Burckhardt model): Established tire-road friction model
- Table 1 (Surface coefficients): Empirical data, necessary

**Verdict:** All equations are standard control theory formulations. No decorative formalism.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Transforming 3D Models to Semantic Web Representation
paper.abstract: Rule-based X3D to RDF transformation using RML. SPARQL queries for validation and reverse transformation. Ontology-based 3D model representation for semantic web applications.

verdict.summary: Applied semantic web engineering paper. No mathematical formalism - uses code listings (RML rules, SPARQL queries) which are appropriate for the domain. Transformation rules are functional specifications, not decorative. Tables show query results for validation.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** X3D to RDF transformation
**Level:** 2 (Applied semantic web engineering)
**Rationale:** Rule-based data transformation, standard technology application.

### Necessity Test
- RML rule listings (1-5): Necessary - functional specifications
- SPARQL queries: Necessary - validation and reverse transformation
- Tables 1-5: Query results, necessary for demonstration
- No mathematical notation misuse

**Verdict:** Paper uses code/query listings instead of mathematical formalism. Appropriate for software engineering domain.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: New Opportunities Model for Monitoring, Analyzing and Forecasting the Official Statistics on Coronavirus Disease Pandemic
paper.abstract: COVID-19 pandemic modeling using parabolic regression. Proposes 'new opportunities model' extending SI model with transition detection. Applied to Russia, Romania, Moldova, and Campania region.

verdict.summary: Epidemiological modeling paper. SI model equations (5)-(8) are standard. Dataset definition (Eq. 1), secondary data calculations (Eq. 2-4) are necessary. New opportunities model steps (NO1-NO4) appropriately formalized. Phase plane visualizations support analysis.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** COVID-19 pandemic forecasting
**Level:** 3 (Mathematical modeling with optimization)
**Rationale:** Extends classical SI model with regime change detection.

### Necessity Test
- Eq. 1 (Dataset definition): Necessary for data specification
- Eq. 2-4 (Secondary data): Simple arithmetic, necessary
- Eq. 5-6 (SI model): Standard epidemiological formulation
- Eq. 7-8 (Solution): Analytical solution, necessary for prediction
- Eq. 10 (Parameter estimation): Necessary for model fitting
- Steps SI1-SI3, NO1-NO4: Algorithm specification, appropriate

**Verdict:** Formalism is standard for mathematical modeling. Ball trajectory analogy is pedagogical, not decorative.

### Issues
None identified.

paper.title: Linear Voltage Controlled Oscillator Implementation in Electronically Variable Immittances
paper.abstract: CFA-MMCC based voltage-tunable inductor and FDNR design. Applications to selective filters and LC-type linear VCO. PSPICE simulation and hardware verification up to 13.6MHz.

verdict.summary: Analog circuit design paper with appropriate formalism. Table 2 (L/D realization) specifies component selection and immittance functions - necessary for design. Equations (3)-(7) for parasitic analysis are standard circuit theory. PSPICE and hardware validation included.

papers.provenance:

### Necessity Test

- Eq. 3-4 (Parasitic effects on L/D): Necessary for accuracy analysis
- Eq. 5-7 (Modified immittance): Standard circuit analysis

paper.title: Information: From Cognitive-Sentient Exploration of Reality to Predictive Big Data Assisted Informational Era
paper.abstract: Philosophical paper on information in consciousness and living structures. Proposes Informational Model of Consciousness (IMC) and Informational System of Human Body (ISHB). Discusses COVID-19 prediction using big data.

flag_details.FORMALISM_THEATER: Equations (1)-(7) are pseudo-mathematical notation for philosophical concepts. Eq. 1 (entropy/information) is Shannon formula but applied metaphorically. Eq. 2-4 use chemical-like notation (=>) for philosophical 'informatter' concepts. Eq. 5 and 7 define ISHB/ISLS as set notation for list of acronyms - classic decorative formalism. No mathematical operations, no predictions derivable from equations.

verdict.summary: FORMALISM THEATER. Paper presents philosophical speculation about consciousness using pseudo-mathematical notation. Equations are decorative: Eq. 5 'ISHB = (CASI + CDC + IES + MIS + GTS + IGG + IC)' is set notation for a list of acronyms, not a computable relationship. Eq. 2-4 use arrow notation for metaphysical claims about 'informatter'. No empirical validation, no testable predictions. Level 1 concepts dressed in Level 4 notation.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** Philosophy of information and consciousness
**Level:** 1-2 (Philosophical speculation, non-mathematical)
**Rationale:** Discusses consciousness, living structures, archaic philosophy. No computable claims.

### Necessity Test
- Eq. 1 (Shannon formula): Borrowed from information theory, applied metaphorically to consciousness
- Eq. 2-3 ('Informatter' reactions): Pseudo-chemical notation for philosophical concepts
- Eq. 4 (A+B+I structuration): No mathematical meaning, decorative
- Eq. 5 (ISHB definition): Set notation for acronym list - FORMALISM THEATER
- Eq. 7 (ISLS definition): Same pattern - acronyms in equation format

**Verdict:** DISQUALIFIED. Level 1 philosophical content using Level 4 mathematical notation decoratively. Classic formalism theater: equations that cannot be computed, derived from, or validated.

### Issues
- Set notation for simple lists (Definition 5, 7)
- Pseudo-chemical reaction notation for metaphysical claims
- No empirical content despite equation-heavy presentation
- Self-citation heavy (author's prior philosophical work)

paper.title: Development of Microcomputers and Motor Control Software for Steel Pipe Cutting Units
paper.abstract: Microcomputer design for DC motor control in steel pipe cutting. Replaces hydraulic system with DC motor and thyristor regulator. Real-time control software development.

verdict.summary: Industrial control systems paper. DC motor equations (1)-(3) are standard electromechanical modeling. Transfer function derivation (Eq. 4-6) is necessary for control design. Laplace domain analysis appropriate for control engineering. Practical industrial application.

papers.provenance:
## Formalism Analysis

### Concept Complexity
**Topic:** DC motor control for industrial application
**Level:** 2-3 (Applied control engineering)
**Rationale:** Standard DC motor control with microcomputer implementation.

### Necessity Test
- Eq. 1 (Rotor voltage): Standard DC motor equation
- Eq. 2 (Torque equilibrium): Fundamental mechanics
- Eq. 3 (Excitation): Standard motor modeling
- Eq. 4-6 (Transfer function): Necessary for control design
- Fig. 1 (Speed regulation modes): Necessary operational specification

**Verdict:** Formalism is standard for control engineering. All equations are functional, derived from motor physics.

### Issues
None identified.

batch_notes: Volume 26 Issue 1 (2023) analyzed with RUIN Framework v2.0 and Necessity Test. **1 paper flagged FORMALISM_THEATER and DISQUALIFIED**: gaiseanu2023 uses pseudo-mathematical notation for philosophical speculation about consciousness (set notation for acronym lists, pseudo-chemical notation for metaphysical claims). Remaining 6 papers have domain-appropriate formalism: NLP algorithm design, ABS control, semantic web engineering, pandemic modeling, analog circuits, motor control. Final: 1 CRITICAL (disqualified), 0 LIMITED, 4 ADEQUATE, 2 STRONG.
